title: v2, podman: propagate --network settings
body:
it fixes tests using --net=host

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I think this should happen on the server side not in the client.
Client-side sounds right, but we should try and leave the namespace defaulted whenever possible so the server can autoselect slirp for rootless, and pod mode if in a pod.
should we drop `ContainerCLIOpts.Net` and just pass down strings?

We can definitely parse from string in the client...

Maybe we should move `containers.conf` parsing into the server, and only set network-related stuff in SpecGen if the user explicitly set it. That might be better than what we're doing.
The problem with doing that is the podman --help values start being incorrect.
I think we still display the default in the client code, but we only forward to the user if the flag was set - if it's the default we don't bother
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #5905) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@mheon Does your PR replace the need for this PR?

> I think it does

How so?  I don't see any namespace setting from the CLI being honored.  It is also introducing other regressions (e.g. `--userns=auto` is not working again).
I'll work on the user namespace part and sync with @mheon once it is ready
